search_queries_used: site:dha.gov.ae conjunctivitis eye discharge guideline PDF
candidate_sources_rejected: dacryocystitis-only sources as complete discharge coverage
rejection_reasons: Patient pages are not clinician guidance.
population_applicability: Partial: exact for adult and child conjunctivitis discharge and selected keratitis warnings; not exact for dacryocystitis, epiphora, or every discharge cause.
setting_applicability: Direct for DHA telehealth; partial for in-person corneal examination and microbiology.
UAE_applicability: Verified for DHA-licensed telehealth in Dubai, United Arab Emirates; specialist pathways require clinician review.
recency_verification: DHA Issue 2 and College microbial-keratitis Version 14 were opened on 2026-07-12.
superseded_check: No newer official DHA issue or College version was identified.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped discharge character, crusting, redness, itching, contact history, laterality, contact-lens, and duration fields.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Describes unilateral or bilateral redness, watery, mucus or purulent discharge, crusting, itching, burning, grittiness, foreign-body sensation, contact spread, dry-eye symptoms, and mechanical or chemical insult context.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped vision, pain, photophobia, trauma, contact-lens, corneal, and pupil warning fields.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Lists reduced visual acuity, ciliary flush, photophobia, severe foreign-body sensation, corneal opacity, fixed pupil, severe headache with nausea, post-traumatic red eye, and immune compromise.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped clinician-assessed visual acuity, discharge, conjunctiva, cornea, and pupil fields.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Requires history and visual review for red eye and discharge, distinguishes discharge character, asks about vision, foreign-body sensation, photophobia, trauma and contact lenses, and highlights keratitis risk in contact-lens wearers.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped hygiene, contact-lens, medication, return, and follow-up documentation without doses.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinician-led management, contact-lens hygiene or cessation discussion, response review, infection-control education, and referral for persistent symptoms, reduced acuity, photophobia, corneal opacity, painful red eye, severe headache with nausea, hyphema, trauma, or inability to open the eye; dose tables are not mapped.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped severe pain, photophobia, discharge, blurred vision, and corneal concern fields already mapped to DHA.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports usually unilateral acute redness, moderate or severe pain, photophobia, watering, discharge, blurred vision, corneal opacity or ulcer, and contact-lens, injury, surgery, exposure, dry-eye, or immune-risk context.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports emergency context; no separate legacy item is mapped.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Identifies microbial keratitis as sight-threatening and supports urgent ophthalmology documentation; treatment specifics are not mapped.

unresolved_source_gaps: Dacryocystitis, epiphora, eyelid swelling, fluorescein, eye movement, red reflex, fundoscopy, visual fields, and fixed medication selection remain unsupported.
Qualified clinician review has not been completed.
